mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and signals easier financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is trending down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than not when FEDFUNDS is falling for 3 consecutive months, or if rolling 2-year rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in PAYEMS after a labor-market trough signals improving household income and future consumption, which lifts equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises above its 3-month moving average while UNRATE is flat or declining
failure_condition: Reject if triggered instances fail to show positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal loses directional accuracy below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger retail spending and near-term revenue momentum for cyclicals and large-cap firms, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly-style 5-day forward returns are not higher after RSAFS growth surprises, or if the sign hit rate falls below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while CPIAUCSL is also rising, the economy is overheated and inflation pressure forces tighter financial conditions, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if high-industrial-output/high-inflation triggers are followed by positive median SPX returns, or if the conditional relationship becomes non-negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE reflects deteriorating labor income and weaker consumption, so the broad equity market should fall over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise episodes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for prior 3-month market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals weakening residential demand and future construction activity, which reduces cyclical growth expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average for two consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if housing-deterioration triggers are not followed by below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if median forward returns are not negative in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL signals policy tightening under inflation pressure, which compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation positive
failure_condition: Reject if tight-policy/inflation episodes do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant and sign-inconsistent over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and PAYEMS is still expanding, broad real activity is improving enough to support earnings growth, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDP-and-payroll combinations fail to deliver positive median 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint signal underperforms either proxy alone in walk-forward testing.
